data.title: Valmont Industries (VMI): Buy Rating Reaffirmed as Transient Brazil Headwinds Contrast With Solid 2026 Earnings Outlook
data.description:
William Blair analyst Brian Drab has maintained their bullish stance on VMI stock, giving a Buy rating today.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Brian Drab has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that balance near‑term noise with solid longer‑term fundamentals. While fourth‑quarter results were slightly shy of expectations because of weaker irrigation performance in Brazil, infrastructure operations tracked in line, and the profit shortfall was partly tied to discrete legal and credit items that are not expected to recur at the same magnitude.

Drab also highlights that unusual tax benefits and Brazil‑related charges essentially offset, leaving the underlying earnings power largely intact. Management’s 2026 outlook, calling for low‑to‑mid single‑digit sales growth and a healthy adjusted EPS range, aligns with prior market expectations and supports a constructive view on earnings growth, underpinning his favorable valuation stance and Buy recommendation on VMI.

In another report released today, J.P. Morgan also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $510.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: RioCan: Solid Property Fundamentals Offset by Higher Interest Costs and Full Valuation Justify Hold Rating
data.description:
Mark Rothschild, an analyst from Canaccord Genuity, maintained the Hold rating on RioCan Real Estate Investment. The associated price target was raised to C$20.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mark Rothschild has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to RioCan’s mixed financial and operating outlook. While the trust is showing solid same-property NOI growth in its core commercial portfolio, this strength is being counterbalanced by higher interest costs that are pressuring core FFO per unit, which declined despite generally healthy property fundamentals.

At the same time, RioCan’s units trade at a modest premium to his updated NAV estimate and at valuation multiples that are only slightly below or in line with peers once non-recurring items are adjusted for, limiting upside from the current price. The continued softness in the small residential segment, along with ongoing asset sales there, also tempers the overall growth profile, leading Rothschild to see the current risk‑reward as fairly balanced rather than compelling enough for a Buy recommendation.

data.title: Gilead Sciences: Strong HIV Growth Priced In, Diversification Still Limited Justifying Hold Rating
data.description:
Maxim Group analyst Michael Okunewitch has maintained their neutral stance on GILD stock, giving a Hold rating on February 11.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Michael Okunewitch has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Gilead’s current performance and valuation. He notes that the company’s HIV franchise, led by Biktarvy, Descovy and the newer PrEP drug Yeztugo, is delivering solid growth and appears well insulated from competition, with additional upside from longer-acting formulations that should help sustain modest market expansion.

At the same time, he argues that much of this growth story is already embedded in the share price, as Gilead trades at a premium to large-cap biopharma peers and is near all-time highs. Oncology and liver disease products offer incremental opportunities, but he views these franchises as too small, for now, to transform the company’s reliance on HIV, which still represents the majority of sales, leading him to conclude that the current outlook is adequately reflected in the valuation.

In another report released on February 11, Goldman Sachs also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $125.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Verisk: Robust Q4 Beat, Resilient Outlook, and Transitory Revenue Pressures Underpin Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Andrew Nicholas has reiterated their bullish stance on VRSK stock, giving a Buy rating on February 11.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Andrew Nicholas has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, starting with Verisk’s fourth‑quarter performance that materially exceeded both his projections and broader market expectations on revenue, margins, and earnings. The company delivered robust organic growth, particularly in its subscription business, while disciplined cost management translated into stronger‑than‑modeled profitability.

Nicholas also highlights that management’s 2026 guidance, once adjusted for the Verisk Marketing Solutions divestiture, aligns with or slightly exceeds his prior forecasts and appears better than the market had feared, especially after the stock’s notable year‑to‑date pullback. He sees the recent results and outlook as evidence of the business’s resilience through cycles, views current pressures on transactional revenue as transitory, and remains confident that Verisk’s competitive position and pricing power will hold up even as the industry evolves and AI adoption accelerates.

In another report released on February 11, Wells Fargo also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $237.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
